destinations.evidence: After system-call execution, returned state is filtered to retain only the called contract address, dropping caller and beneficiary touches.

equivalence_argument: The state-retention filter is applied after the temporary system-call execution and before returning ResultAndState, so the output state surface is contract-only even if revm touched caller or beneficiary accounts internally.
notes: Mapped to the nodebuilder RitualEvm system-call cleanup.
rationale: The hunk explicitly retains only entries whose address equals the target contract to match upstream's system-call cleanup behavior. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: After transact_system_call completes, its ResultAndState must retain only state changes for the called contract address, dropping touched caller and block beneficiary accounts from the returned state.
suggested_falsification_tests: Run a system call that touches only target storage and inspect ResultAndState; assert it contains the target contract state but not the caller or block beneficiary accounts even if revm touched them internally.
summary: System-call ResultAndState is filtered to the target contract only.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Run a system call that causes caller/beneficiary touches and assert ResultAndState contains only the target contract address.
title: System call state output is contract-only
